summary: I explored the Pipeliner repo to scope the "add dark mode" ask and wrote factual discovery notes to `.pipeliner/phases/01-define/main/codebase-explorer/output/discovery_notes.md`. Key findings: the app is a server-rendered Rails 8 + Hotwire + **Tailwind v4** (import-map JS, no Node build step) codebase with **zero existing dark-mode/theme/user-preference infrastructure** — no `dark:` variants, no theme CSS (the Tailwind entry is a bare `@import "tailwindcss"`), no settings column on `users`, no preferences route/controller, and no custom Stimulus controllers yet. Light-mode colors are hard-coded inline across ~23 of 28 views, the sole centralized exception being status-badge tones in `status_helper.rb`. The notes map the full surface area (Tailwind config, both layouts, all views, a new toggle + first Stimulus controller, persistence, and FOUC prevention), list open questions (per-browser vs. cross-device persistence, 2- vs. 3-state toggle, toggle placement including sidebar-less auth pages, refactor-first vs. inline `dark:`), and capture constraints — most notably that `guides/ui-style-guide.md` explicitly says "Dark mode later — don't hand-roll it per view," directing a central/token-based implementation, plus Tailwind v4's need for an explicit `@custom-variant` for class-based toggling and the CSP nonce caveat for any inline pre-paint script.
